articles.title: Europe is pushing back on Washington’s chip war
articles.title_en: Europe is pushing back on Washington’s chip war
articles.summary: As ASML CEO Christophe Fouquet told TechCrunch in May, what China can currently buy are older-generation deep ultraviolet tools — gear first shipped about a decade ago — the same machines the MATCH Act would now put off limits.

articles.title: Former Infosys chief has a new startup that wants to challenge the IT services world
articles.title_en: Former Infosys chief has a new startup that wants to challenge the IT services world
articles.summary: Backed by Mayfield and Aramco Ventures, Vishal Sikka’s new venture brings together veterans from SAP, Infosys, and VianAI.

articles.title: Cerebras stock plunges after earnings as CEO says margin outlook was misunderstood
articles.title_en: Cerebras stock plunges after earnings as CEO says margin outlook was misunderstood
articles.summary: In its first earnings report since going public, the AI chipmaker forecast a narrower gross margin in its core business, scaring investors.

articles.title: OpenAI and Broadcom announce chip designed for LLM inference at scale
articles.title_en: OpenAI and Broadcom announce chip designed for LLM inference at scale
articles.summary: The silicon race is heating up amid the struggle to keep up with demand.

articles.title: AI was supposed to kill engineering jobs, but new data suggests they’re the most resilient
articles.title_en: AI was supposed to kill engineering jobs, but new data suggests they’re the most resilient
articles.summary: While AI dominates the layoff narrative, engineers are actually making up a larger share of new hires, according to SignalFire data.

articles.title: AI researchers continue to leave Google for its rivals
articles.title_en: AI researchers continue to leave Google for its rivals
articles.summary: Top AI researchers Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel are leaving Google for Anthropic, following departures from top scientists Noam Shazeer and John Jumper.

articles.title: The memory chip crunch is paying off for this US company
articles.title_en: The memory chip crunch is paying off for this US company
articles.summary: Revenue quadrupled to $41.45 billion compared with the same period a year ago. The company's profit, meanwhile, rose from $1.88 billion to an incredible $28.2 billion year-over-year.

articles.title: Companies are scrambling to stop employees from maxing out AI budgets with small tasks
articles.title_en: Companies are scrambling to stop employees from maxing out AI budgets with small tasks
articles.summary: The tokenmaxxing era was brief. We now appear to be entering the era of token rationing.

articles.title: Gradium Launches stt-translate and s2s-translate, Real-Time Speech Translation Models Beating gpt-realtime-translate on Accuracy and Latency
articles.title_en: Gradium Launches stt-translate and s2s-translate, Real-Time Speech Translation Models Beating gpt-realtime-translate on Accuracy and Latency
articles.summary: Gradium released two real-time speech translation models, stt-translate and s2s-translate, covering English, French, German, Spanish, and Portuguese across 20 language pairs. The models collapse the standard three-model cascade into two, pairing single-pass transcription-and-translation with a Gradium TTS stage over one duplex WebSocket. Gradium reports a better accuracy-latency tradeoff than gpt-realtime-translate and gemini-3.5-live-translate, plus output voice selection and cloning. The post

articles.title: Congresswoman denies staff used AI to write defense funding amendment
articles.title_en: Congresswoman denies staff used AI to write defense funding amendment
articles.summary: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-FL) says her staff used AI for "spellcheck" in an amendment summary for a major defense bill, but denies it was used for the bill text itself and says "NO Legislation is ever drafted with AI." Luna issued the response after accounts on X began sharing screenshots of an amendment summary […]

articles.title: How to Design an OpenHarness Style Agent Runtime with Tools, Memory, Permissions, Skills, and Multi-Agent Coordination
articles.title_en: How to Design an OpenHarness Style Agent Runtime with Tools, Memory, Permissions, Skills, and Multi-Agent Coordination
articles.summary: In this tutorial, we build an OpenHarness style agent harness from scratch to see how a practical agent system works. We recreate the core building blocks: tool use, typed tool schemas, permissions, lifecycle hooks, memory, skills, context compaction, retry logic, cost tracking, and multi-agent coordination. We expose the full control flow instead of treating the framework as a black box. We keep everything runnable so we can experiment without API keys or extra infrastructure. The post How to D
